texts.full_text: arrived. HARPER: They walk to here - and so you can see hundreds of refugees under the olive groves there, and this is what is terrifying because there's just so many kids. AMOS: Harper is here to find out about the battles across the border. The fighting determines refugee flows. He also wants to know how many Syrians are returning home. In April, some 10,000 headed back after rebels took the town of Dael in southern Syria and opened a road to this border. In a tent crowded with Syrian families, the stories come all at once. Most are from the town of Jassim, north of Dael, where rebels and regime loyalists are still fighting. One young mother from Jassim, who wouldn't give her name, said an artillery shell hit her home. She packed up the kids two days ago for the

texts.full_text: ay For College." Many people are questioning whether the high cost justifies its value. That means fewer students can afford to go, which creates less demand from employers." Bowe's view reflects an increasingly critical attitude toward higher education in America as it faces unprecedented pressure and scrutiny. Rising Costs The average annual price of tuition at a four-year private college has jumped nearly 300% over the past three decades. The cost is even more daunting for public colleges, which have seen their prices rise by about two-thirds during that time. In addition to higher costs," says Bowe , "there's also growing concern among students and parents who are paying off large debts after graduation." Accordingly" it makes sense they're asking the question: Is a degree worth so much money?" Mounting Debts The average student loan debt in America now exceeds $30,000. And that figure is rising more quickly than inflation. That kind of financial burden can be daunting for students who are just beginning to make their way into adulthood," says Bowe . "They have the potential and talent but they're not sure whether it's worth taking on so much debt." Workforce Preparation There has been a growing sense that preparation in higher education need no longer take four years. That can be seen from an increasing number of employers who are hiring workers with less formal training. For example," says Bowe , "many companies have started looking for candidates not just because they're willing to work hard but also those whose skills and experience fit the job." This shift means that more people may find themselves better off pursuing two-year degrees, trade school programs or even apprenticeships. The Noble Failure As Americans' faith in higher education reacts negatively," says Bowe , "it's important for institutions to take a closer look at their value proposition and make sure they're providing the skills that employers need.

texts.full_text: COA Trophy Clubs Publications Grants Library SHELLS Conchology Conservation Collecting FAQS Learn Junonia by S. Peter Dance Sacheverell Sitwell may have been the first to point out that seashells enliven the foreground scenery of several of Audubon's bird plates. Scattered on the dull rocks and sands against which Audubon portrays some of his seabirds, they reminded Sitwell of the folding double frontispiece to Utamaro's Presents of the Ebb Tide which portrays a group of ladies walking on a shell-strewn shore. texts.full_text: s that the CA attests to the entity's identity. A public-key infrastructure implements digital certificates that allow public keys to be distributed in a hierarchy of trusted relationships. A Certification Authority (CA) acts as a trusted third party that can issue signed certificates on behalf of another entity on a network. The CA uses its own private key to encrypt the certificate, which contains the entity's public key together with other information about
